retrieval.query_summary: Analysis of Crash 1000 Index for a bearish intraday setup based on SMC and timeframe alignment.

factors.name: Macro bias alignment
factors.notes: Macro data is neutral/unavailable, which is treated as non-blocking.

factors.name: HTF structure aligned
factors.notes: D1, H12, H8, H6, H4, H3, and H1 timeframes are all aligned bearish, confirming the dominant trend.

factors.name: MTF BOS or ChoCH confirmed
factors.notes: H1 timeframe confirms a recent bearish CHoCH and BMS, validating the entry direction.

factors.name: Valid Structural Entry Support
factors.notes: A valid H1 Bearish Order Block is present at the entry zone [5515.963, 5532.739].

factors.name: Liquidity sweep into entry zone
factors.notes: The candidate confirms a sweep of Buy-Side Liquidity (BSL) at 5570.955 before the structural break, indicating a stop hunt.

factors.name: COT alignment
factors.notes: COT data is not available for this instrument.

factors.name: Wyckoff phase supports direction
factors.notes: The BSL sweep followed by a sharp breakdown (BMS) is characteristic of an Upthrust/UTAD and the beginning of a Markdown phase, supporting a short position.

factors.name: No high-impact news within 30 minutes
factors.notes: No high-impact news events are scheduled.

factors.name: Minimum R:R achievable
factors.notes: The R:R to the final TP target is approximately 15.4R, well above the 1:3 minimum for Intraday style.

cot_signal.summary: No COT data available for this instrument.

evidence.content_preview: Smart Money Concepts (SMC) | Order flow, institutional footprints, liquidity engineering | 4H / 1H — entry precision | Primary

raw_response.explainable_reasoning: The analysis identifies a high-probability short setup on Crash 1000 Index based on strong multi-timeframe bearish alignment. The dominant trend is bearish from the Daily down to the M5 timeframe. A key H1 CHOCH+BMS+SH+RTO pattern (candidate ID ...253ea558) has formed, confirming a shift to bearish order flow after sweeping buy-side liquidity at 5570.955. The current M1 bullish price action is interpreted as a counter-trend pullback (inducement) into a high-quality H1 Bearish Order Block located at [5515.963, 5532.739]. The setup achieves a confluence score of 9/10, grading it as A+. A limit order will be placed within the OB, with a structural stop loss above its high at 5535.0. Take profit levels are set at key sell-side liquidity pools on the M15, H1, and D1 timeframes, offering an excellent R:R ratio. This trade aligns with the HTF narrative and capitalizes on an LTF pullback for an optimal entry.

htf_bias.notes: Strong bearish alignment from D1 down to H1. The overall trend is confirmed as BEARISH. While W1 shows a bullish structure, the current D1 price action is a clear bearish leg, which dictates the intraday directional bias.

mtf_bias.notes: H1 timeframe has confirmed a bearish shift with both a CHoCH at 5533.759 and a subsequent BMS at 5484.535. This establishes clear bearish order flow for an intraday perspective.

evidence.content_preview: SL must be structural — placed beyond the invalidation level of the setup (beyond OB, beyond QML, beyond swing high/low)

stop_loss.reason: Structurally placed above the high of the H1 Bearish Order Block, providing a buffer against wicks.

take_profits.basis: Nearest significant Sell-Side Liquidity (SSL) pool from the M15 timeframe.
Major H1 swing low at 5481.185, a primary draw on liquidity.
Deeper structural swing low from the D1 timeframe at 5357.275, representing the HTF liquidity target.

evidence.content_preview: Wyckoff Theory | Phase identification, composite operator logic | 1W / 1D — directional context only | Context
